activities_projects.title: Detailed Market Chain Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze each stage of a shirt’s journey in depth
steps: Research the processes of ginning, spinning, weaving, and tailoring
Write 2–3 sentences explaining the significance of each step
Identify challenges faced by workers at each stage
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop deeper understanding of production processes and labor issues

activities_projects.title: Cost and Profit Calculation
activities_projects.objective: Understand economics behind pricing a shirt
steps: Estimate costs at each stage: raw material, labor, transport, retail markup
Calculate approximate final price and profit margins
Discuss why prices increase from farmer to retailer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about economics of trade and profit distribution
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of calculations and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study: Worker Challenges
activities_projects.objective: Explore labor conditions in production and trade
steps: Research working conditions of cotton farmers, weavers, and factory workers
Write a brief case study highlighting one challenge for each group
Suggest solutions for improving fairness and safety
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop empathy and understanding of social issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research and reflection (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Supply Chain Infographic
steps: Design an infographic showing the journey of a shirt with icons and arrows
Include workers, costs, and market stages
Add one fun fact or statistic per stage
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop visual literacy and critical presentation skills

activities_projects.title: Debate: Who Benefits the Most?
activities_projects.objective: Critically assess inequalities in the market
steps: Divide class into groups representing farmers, traders, workers, retailers
Research data to support your arguments
Hold a structured debate and vote for the most convincing group
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve reasoning, research, and public speaking skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and teamwork (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interview and Report: Local Clothing Market
activities_projects.objective: Connect chapter learning with real-life market practices
steps: Visit a local shop or market
Interview seller about sourcing, costs, and pricing of shirts
Prepare a report comparing findings with chapter content
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to relate textbook concepts with real-world markets
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of observation and quality of report (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fair Trade Research Poster
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of ethical trading
steps: Research one fair trade organization or brand
Create a poster showing how it ensures fair wages and working conditions
Include statistics or examples to support your poster
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand and promote ethical practices
activities_projects.evaluation: Research quality, accuracy, and creativity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Storytelling: A Shirt’s Perspective
steps: Write a story from the perspective of a shirt
Include all stakeholders it ‘meets’ along the journey
Discuss social, economic, and ethical aspects in your story
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students combine creativity with critical analysis

activities_projects.title: Compare Markets Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand differences between local and global trade
steps: Compare how shirts are sold locally vs exported globally
Identify differences in pricing, workers’ pay, and market scale
Write observations in a table format
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop global awareness and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness and clarity of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mini Quiz with Analysis
steps: Prepare 10 medium-level questions from the chapter (include why/how questions)
Ask classmates and record answers
Discuss reasoning behind each answer collectively
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students strengthen knowledge and critical thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Question quality and explanation depth (10 marks)
